evals.name: saas-startup-roadmap
evals.prompt: I'm CTO of a 12-person SaaS startup. We're spread across 8 initiatives right now: core product improvements, a new mobile app, API partner integrations, SOC2 compliance, redesigning onboarding, building an analytics dashboard, migrating to k8s, and writing a technical blog. Revenue is $180k MRR but growth has flatlined at 3% month-over-month. Our runway is 14 months. I need to figure out what to cut and what to double down on. Can you do a Pareto audit on our roadmap?
evals.expected_output: Pareto decomposition table with 8 activities ranked by contribution to MRR growth. VITAL_20 should identify 2-3 items (likely core product + onboarding or API integrations). TRIVIAL_80 should flag blog, k8s migration, analytics dashboard. Unconventional reframes should challenge at least one 'obvious' priority. Prune list with freed engineering hours estimate.

assertions.name: pareto_table_present
assertions.description: Output contains a Pareto decomposition table

assertions.name: vital_20_tagged
assertions.description: Activities are tagged with VITAL_20 tier

assertions.name: trivial_80_tagged
assertions.description: Activities are tagged with TRIVIAL_80 tier

evals.name: personal-time-audit
evals.prompt: I tracked my time for 2 weeks. Here's where my hours went: meetings (22h/wk), Slack/email (15h/wk), deep coding (8h/wk), code review (6h/wk), 1:1s with reports (4h/wk), interviewing candidates (3h/wk), writing design docs (2h/wk), exercise (1.5h/wk). I'm a senior staff engineer and I feel like I'm not shipping anything meaningful. My promotion packet is due in 6 months. Run a Pareto audit on my time.
evals.expected_output: Pareto decomposition showing deep coding + design docs as VITAL_20 despite low current hours. Meetings + Slack/email as TRIVIAL_80 consuming 37h/wk. Joy×value matrix since this is personal domain. Non-linear causal analysis showing that meetings generate Slack threads which generate more meetings (feedback loop). Concrete prune: reduce meetings to <10h, batch Slack to 2 windows/day.

assertions.name: hedonistic_mode_active
assertions.description: Hedonistic mode engages — addresses guilt-driven activity

assertions.name: feedback_loop_identified
assertions.description: Non-linear mode identifies feedback loops in time use

evals.name: codebase-module-audit
evals.prompt: Our monorepo has 14 modules: auth, billing, notifications, admin-panel, search, recommendations, analytics, reporting, user-profiles, permissions, audit-log, feature-flags, email-templates, onboarding-wizard. Bug reports from last quarter: auth (47), billing (31), search (22), recommendations (3), notifications (18), admin-panel (2), analytics (8), reporting (12), user-profiles (6), permissions (15), audit-log (1), feature-flags (4), email-templates (9), onboarding-wizard (7). We have 4 engineers available for a stability sprint. Where should they focus?
evals.expected_output: Pareto decomposition using bug counts as output metric. auth + billing + search = 100 bugs (54% of 185 total). Add notifications + permissions = 80%. These 5 modules are VITAL_20. Concrete allocation: 2 engineers on auth, 1 on billing, 1 split search/notifications. Prune: audit-log, admin-panel, recommendations get zero sprint allocation. Non-linear: check if auth bugs cascade into permissions bugs (shared dependency).

assertions.name: cascade_analysis
assertions.description: Non-linear mode identifies potential bug cascades between modules
